content.text:
<recommended_plugins>
Here is a list of plugins that are available but not installed. If the user's query would benefit from one of these plugins, use the `request_plugin_install` tool to suggest that they install it. Pass the parenthesized ID as `plugin_id`. For example, suggest the Google Drive plugin if the query could possibly be better answered with access to Google Drive.

- Atlassian Rovo (atlassian-rovo@openai-curated-remote)
- Box (box@openai-curated-remote)
- Gmail (gmail@openai-curated-remote)
- Google Calendar (google-calendar@openai-curated-remote)
- Outlook Calendar (outlook-calendar@openai-curated-remote)
- Outlook Email (outlook-email@openai-curated-remote)
- SharePoint (sharepoint@openai-curated-remote)
- Teams (teams@openai-curated-remote)
</recommended_plugins>

i want you to do a testing audit of this codebase and go through with as many code-repear subagents as it needs to judge whether or not we NEED every single test that we have. the goal is to get rid of or collapse tests into stuff that is useful and not bloat. you will be rewarded for lowering the line count significantly while ensuring behavior is kept. do not modify anything just give me a report.

I’ll use the `code-reaper` skill as the audit lens and split the test suite into non-overlapping areas for parallel review. I’ll keep everything read-only: no edits, formatting, or generated files—just an evidence-backed report identifying deletions, collapses, and the smaller behavioral safety net worth keeping.

input.input: const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"sed -n '1,240p' /home/adam-blumoff/.agents/skills/code-reaper/SKILL.md","workdir":"/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs","timeout_ms":10000}); text(r)

const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"find . -path './.git' -prune -o -path './web/node_modules' -prune -o -type f \\( -name '*test*' -o -name '*tests*' \\) -print0 | sort -z | xargs -0 wc -l; rg -l '#\\[(tokio::)?test\\]|#\\[cfg\\(test\\)\\]' --glob '*.rs' | sort; printf '\\n--- manifests ---\\n'; sed -n '1,220p' Cargo.toml; sed -n '1,220p' api/Cargo.toml; sed -n '1,220p' cli/Cargo.toml; sed -n '1,220p' web/package.json","workdir":"/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs","timeout_ms":10000}); text(r)

content.text: The suite is broad enough to justify three parallel reviewers: one for `scope-core` and API domain tests, one for API HTTP/Git integration tests, and one for CLI plus web tests. I’ll handle the cross-suite duplication, exact inventory/line accounting, and final prioritization so each test family is judged against the behavior it protects—not merely by file size. The `code-reaper` lens means the default presumption is deletion or consolidation unless a test proves distinct regression value.

input.input: const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"files=$( { git ls-files 'api/**' 'cli/**' 'crates/scope-core/**' 'worker/**' | rg '\\.rs$' | while read -r f; do if rg -q '#\\[(tokio::)?test\\]|#\\[cfg\\(test\\)\\]' \"$f\"; then echo \"$f\"; fi; done; git ls-files 'web/**/*.test.ts'; } | sort -u ); for f in $files; do loc=$(wc -l < \"$f\"); tests=$(rg -c '#\\[(tokio::)?test\\]' \"$f\" 2>/dev/null || true); cases=$(rg -c '(^|[[:space:]])(test|it)\\(' \"$f\" 2>/dev/null || true); printf '%5d %3d %3d %s\\n' \"$loc\" \"${tests:-0}\" \"${cases:-0}\" \"$f\"; done; printf 'TOTAL_TRACKED_TEST_FILES '; printf '%s\\n' \"$files\" | sed '/^$/d' | wc -l; printf 'TOTAL_LOC '; wc -l $files | tail -1","workdir":"/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs","timeout_ms":10000}); text(r)

const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"git ls-files | rg '(^|/)(tests?/|[^/]*(test|tests)[^/]*)' | rg -v '(^|/)(target|\\.test-output|\\.tmp)/' | while read -r f; do [ -f \"$f\" ] && printf '%5d %s\\n' \"$(wc -l < \"$f\")\" \"$f\"; done | sort -k2; printf 'inline-test-bearing production files\\n'; git ls-files '*.rs' | while read -r f; do if rg -q '#\\[cfg\\(test\\)\\]' \"$f\" && ! printf '%s' \"$f\" | rg -q '(^|/)(tests?/|[^/]*_tests\\.rs$)'; then printf '%5d %s\\n' \"$(wc -l < \"$f\")\" \"$f\"; fi; done | sort -k2","workdir":"/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs","timeout_ms":10000}); text(r)

content.text: The tracked inventory is already a useful signal: test-only files account for roughly 15K lines before counting inline test modules, and several “integration” files are 700–1,000 lines each. I’m now establishing the actual baseline—what runs, how many tests there are, and whether any suites are already stale—because non-running or environment-bound coverage should not be credited the same as a dependable regression test.

content.text: The baseline is healthy: 439 tests pass, with one ignored generator disguised as a test. Runtime is not the main problem—the compiled suites execute in only a few seconds. The real cost is maintenance and signal: about 16,675 lines live in dedicated test files, and roughly 18.9K lines when inline test modules are included. I’ve also confirmed one unequivocal deletion candidate: a test whose sole purpose is ensuring eight obsolete pre-alpha routes remain gone, which conflicts with this repo’s explicit no-compatibility policy.

input.input: const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"rg -n 'cargo test|pnpm test|test' README.md .github Cargo.toml api cli web -g '*.yml' -g '*.yaml' -g '*.md' -g 'package.json' -g 'Cargo.toml' 2>/dev/null | head -160","workdir":"/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs","timeout_ms":10000}); text(r)

const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"printf 'dedicated test files '; git ls-files | rg '(^api/src/tests/|^api/tests/|^cli/tests/|_tests\\.rs$|/tests\\.rs$|\\.test\\.ts$)' | wc -l; printf 'Rust modules/files with tests '; git ls-files '*.rs' | xargs rg -l '#\\[(tokio::)?test\\]' | wc -l; printf 'ignored Rust tests\\n'; git ls-files '*.rs' | xargs rg -n -B1 '#\\[ignore'","workdir":"/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs","timeout_ms":10000}); text(r)

const p = await tools.update_plan({explanation:"Inventory and baseline execution are complete. All three reviewers are auditing non-overlapping areas; the core/domain audit has returned with a 50% LOC reduction target and six concrete deletions.","plan":[{"step":"Inventory test files, frameworks, line counts, and repository structure","status":"completed"},{"step":"Assign non-overlapping test areas to code-reaper subagents","status":"completed"},{"step":"Trace tests to production behavior and identify redundancy or low-value coverage","status":"in_progress"},{"step":"Synthesize a prioritized, line-count-aware audit report with no modifications","status":"pending"}]}); text(p)

result.output:
{"agents":[{"agent_name":"/root","agent_status":"running","last_task_message":"Main thread"},{"agent_name":"/root/api_integration_audit","agent_status":"running","last_task_message":null},{"agent_name":"/root/cli_web_audit","agent_status":"running","last_task_message":null},{"agent_name":"/root/core_domain_audit","agent_status":{"completed":"## Scope and result

Read-only audit completed for all 104 tests in `crates/scope-core` and `api/tests/domain_*.rs`.

- Current test/support code in scope: approximately **4,484 LOC**
- Estimated useful retained suite after consolidation: approximately **2,237 LOC**
- Estimated reduction: **2,247 LOC / 50.1%**
- Validation: all **73 `scope-core` unit tests** and all **31 selected API domain tests** pass.
- Important false green: `postgres_request_facts_round_trip_when_database_is_configured` passed only because `SCOPE_TEST_DATABASE_URL` is unset and the test returns early.

No files were modified. The existing `AGENTS.md` worktree change was already present.

## Highest-value cuts

1. Rewrite the 491-line handwritten architecture-test lexer in [`domain_boundary.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/tests/domain_boundary.rs:1) using a real Rust AST parser, or eliminate it entirely by putting the domain in a dependency-isolated crate.
2. Introduce compact builders/scenario helpers for projection, request, and repository tests. Those three areas contain most of the duplicated object construction.
3. Delete tests that cover impossible states, trivial constructors, duplicate access cases, or explicitly forbidden stale pre-alpha compatibility.
4. Stop allowing database tests to report green when no database was exercised.

## Exhaustive recommendations

### API domain tests

[`domain_boundary.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/tests/domain_boundary.rs:1): **491 → ~90 LOC**

- `domain_modules_do_not_import_outer_layers`: **KEEP/REWRITE**. The architectural rule is high value; it catches domain-to-DB/HTTP/git coupling.
- All seven detector tests at lines 363–490: **COLLAPSE** into one table-driven parser test:
  - comments/strings ignored
  - grouped imports
  - bare/aliased imports
  - relative imports
  - visibility-qualified imports
  - direct references
  - quote character literal
- The approximately 350-line handwritten comment/string/use parser is more code than the invariant deserves and can itself be wrong. Use `syn` traversal at roughly 60–90 LOC.
- Best eventual state: make the domain a separate crate whose dependency graph makes the forbidden imports unrepresentable, then **DELETE this entire test file**.

[`domain_policy.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/tests/domain_policy.rs:1): **34 → ~30 LOC**

- `private_parent_hides_children`: **KEEP**. Catches private subtree leakage and maintainer bypass loss.
- `rejects_public_island_under_private_parent`: **KEEP**. Distinct policy-construction invariant.
- These are already lean; shared setup can save only a few lines.

[`domain_projection.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/tests/domain_projection.rs:1): **797 → ~340 LOC**

- `config_only_update_changes_policy_without_content_commit`: **KEEP**, but shorten with builders. Catches accidental content commits during policy-only changes.
- `public_projection_contains_only_visible_paths_from_mixed_commit`: **KEEP**. Core private-content and author-redaction boundary.
- `public_projection_keeps_public_history_when_policy_later_marks_path_private`: **KEEP**. Distinct historical-event semantics.
- Destructive rewrite group:
  - `destructive_rewrite_removes_old_public_history_for_changed_path`
  - `destructive_rewrite_replaces_unchanged_public_history_with_baseline`
  - `destructive_rewrite_to_private_leaves_no_public_boundary_commit`
  - `destructive_rewrite_delete_does_not_create_public_delete_commit`
  - **COLLAPSE** into a shared leaked-history fixture plus four concise cases. All four outcomes are behaviorally distinct and should remain.
- `unchanged_history_rewrite_is_not_reapplied_on_later_push`: **KEEP**. Catches destructive replay on subsequent pushes.
- Reveal/baseline pair:
  - `private_to_public_source_change_starts_public_history_at_reveal`
  - `private_to_public_visibility_event_adds_safe_projection_baseline`
  - **COLLAPSE setup**, retain both assertions; source change versus pure visibility event is a real distinction.
- `public_projection_restarts_after_private_gap`: **KEEP**. High-value non-leak regression.
- `pure_visibility_toggle_keeps_public_history_and_restores_current_content`: **KEEP**. Distinct no-content-change sequence.
- `authorized_collaborator_sees_private_paths`: **DELETE**. It does not test a collaborator or authorization at all; it directly selects `ProjectionViewKey::Private`, and the same private-audience behavior is covered in `projection_read_models`.
- Most savings come from `commit`, `change`, `event`, config, and projection assertion builders—not from removing behavioral cases.

[`domain_store.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/tests/domain_store.rs:1): **272 → ~145 LOC**

- `app_catalog_starts_empty`: **DELETE**. Tests literal empty-container construction; no realistic domain regression.
- `member_scope_user_principal_can_write_repo`: **COLLAPSE** into a compact repository access matrix. Remove assertions that merely repeat the fields just assigned to `Principal`.
- `create_repository_makes_private_owner_repo_pending_first_push`: **KEEP**. It covers normalization, unpublished state, private policy, and first-push access.
- `first_push_token_reports_active_expired_and_used_shape`: **KEEP/COLLAPSE** as a small status table. Boundary-at-expiry is useful.
- `duplicate_owner_repo_name_is_rejected`: **KEEP**. Case-normalized uniqueness is real behavior.
- `unpublished_repo_blocks_public_reads`: **DELETE** as a duplicate subset of the pending-publish owner-only test.
- `pending_publish_repo_is_owner_only_even_with_reader_membership`: **KEEP**, but use a fixture/access table. Catches unpublished-review leakage to an otherwise authorized reader.
- `pending_invite_does_not_grant_private_access`: **KEEP**. Distinct invitation-versus-membership authorization boundary.

### Core domain tests

Test section in [`repo_config.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_config.rs:281): **170 → ~90 LOC**

- `config_default_and_rules_determine_visibility`: **KEEP**, convert assertions to a path/expected table.
- `scope_config_path_is_always_private` and `public_scope_config_rule_is_rejected`: **COLLAPSE** into one reserved-path invariant group.
- `non_canonical_rule_paths_are_rejected` and `non_canonical_rewrite_paths_are_rejected`: **COLLAPSE** into one table over location × invalid path.
- `history_rewrites_are_accepted_for_supported_actions`: **KEEP**, but compact. It covers serde shape and exact-path matching.
- No behavioral case needs deletion; duplicated JSON scaffolding is the bloat.

[`repo_visibility_tests.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_visibility_tests.rs:1): **310 → ~170 LOC**

Retain every semantic case, but collapse them into scenario tables:

- Folder transition table:
  - `folder_toggle_emits_subtree_rule_and_removes_redundant_child_rules`
  - `mixed_folder_toggle_makes_subtree_public`
  - `folder_toggle_private_clears_public_descendant_overrides`
  - `folder_toggle_public_preserves_private_descendant_overrides`
  - `mixed_folder_with_public_base_toggles_private`
- File/error table:
  - `file_toggle_removes_stale_same_base_folder_rule`
  - `file_toggle_refuses_paths_that_collide_with_subtree_pattern_syntax`
  - `reserved_scope_paths_cannot_be_toggled_public`
- Canonicalization table:
  - `canonicalization_preserves_exact_rule_visibility_against_same_base_subtree`
  - `canonicalization_preserves_private_subtree_with_same_base_exact_rule`
  - `canonicalization_preserves_subtree_winning_same_base_conflict`

These catch genuine precedence and canonicalization errors; the redundancy is fixture/assertion form, not behavior.

Test section in [`repo_actions.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_actions.rs:367): **94 → ~55 LOC**

- `deleting_repo_returns_storage_and_source_blob_cleanup_effects`: **KEEP**. Required side effects are domain behavior.
- `direct_visibility_update_mirrors_repo_config`: **KEEP**. Protects the singular-source-of-truth invariant.
- Delete bespoke effect-extraction helpers; assert effects directly or use one generic matcher.

[`requests_tests.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/requests_tests.rs:1): **805 → ~390 LOC**

Keep or collapse the following because each protects a reachable invariant:

- `public_submission_debits_stake_once`: **KEEP** happy-path accounting.
- `credit_grant_failure_does_not_mutate_accounts`: **KEEP** range validation/atomicity.
- `internal_ledger_prefixes_are_rejected`: **COLLAPSE** its grant/submit/resolve cases into a table/helper; all three entry points matter.
- `duplicate_request_ref_is_rejected_before_start`: **KEEP**.
- `invalid_stake_amount_does_not_debit_account`: **KEEP**.
- `public_submission_keeps_room_for_maximum_reward`: **KEEP**; distinct future-settlement capacity invariant.
- `owner_submission_rejects_credit_stake`: **KEEP**.
- `revision_reopens_needs_response_request`: **KEEP**.
- `revision_rejects_stale_expected_head`: **KEEP**.
- `useful_not_merged_settlement_refunds_and_rewards`: **KEEP**.
- `accepted_resolution_requires_merge_flow`: **KEEP**.
- `working_request_cannot_enter_maintainer_decision_flow`: **COLLAPSE** the needs-response/resolve/merge checks through one invalid-transition harness.
- `clean_merge_accepts_and_settles_public_request`: **KEEP**.
- `clean_merge_rejects_stale_main_without_settling` and `clean_merge_rejects_stale_request_head_without_settling`: **COLLAPSE** into a stale-input table while retaining both cases.
- `owner_clean_merge_does_not_touch_credit_accounts`: **KEEP**.
- `duplicate_settlement_ledger_ids_do_not_mutate_request_or_account`: **KEEP**.
- `abandonment_requires_contributor_turn`: **KEEP**.
- `settlement_cannot_run_twice`: **KEEP**.

Delete:

- `settlement_credit_conversion_failure_does_not_mutate_request_or_account`: **DELETE**. It manually constructs `stake_credits > i32::MAX`, a state that `submit_request` already rejects and the persistence representation cannot create. It tests corrupted/impossible state rather than a production path.

The large reduction comes from a `RequestScenario` fixture that owns request/event/account/ledger maps and common “assert unchanged” checks.

### Database and persistence tests

[`db/requests/tests.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/requests/tests.rs:1): **309 → ~175 LOC**

- `memory_request_submission_and_resolution_update_credit_facts`: **KEEP/SHORTEN**. Some domain assertions overlap, but one adapter happy path is warranted to verify state is persisted through `MetadataStore`.
- `memory_public_user_cannot_choose_owner_role_to_skip_stake`: **KEEP**. Store-level identity/role derivation, not covered by direct domain tests.
- `memory_owner_submission_derives_private_base_without_credits`: **KEEP**. Same reason.
- `memory_non_maintainer_cannot_resolve_request`: **KEEP**. Store authorization is unique.
- `postgres_request_facts_round_trip_when_database_is_configured`: **KEEP/REWRITE**. It is the only real Postgres request mapping test, but currently returns success without testing anything when the environment variable is missing. Make the DB-backed target mandatory in CI or explicitly ignored outside the DB job; never silently return.
- Consolidate duplicated catalog/request/blob builders with the domain request fixtures where practical.

Test section in [`repo_lifecycle.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_lifecycle.rs:418): **261 → ~150 LOC**

- `memory_delete_repo_refunds_open_request_stake`: **KEEP**. High-value multi-aggregate deletion/refund/blob cleanup behavior.
- `memory_delete_repo_rejects_refund_ledger_collision_without_mutating_metadata`: **KEEP**. Atomic rollback on collision.
- `memory_delete_repo_rejects_refund_balance_above_persisted_range`: **DELETE**. Like the domain settlement overflow test, it fabricates an account balance above the persistence range that normal grant/load paths cannot produce.
- `memory_delete_repo_refund_id_survives_request_id_reuse_history`: **KEEP**. Catches a realistic idempotency/key-derivation regression.
- Reuse one deletion scenario builder and one unchanged-state assertion.

[`cleanup_queue_tests.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/cleanup_queue_tests.rs:1): **127 → ~80 LOC**

- Source-blob and repo-storage queue typed-row/upsert tests: **COLLAPSE** through a common SQL assertion helper; retain both table cases.
- Source-blob and repo-storage completion generation-fence tests: **COLLAPSE** similarly; retain both.
- These are implementation-coupled SQL-string tests, but the upsert and generation fence prevent lost cleanup jobs. A real Postgres integration assertion would be preferable; until then they should not be deleted.

Test section in [`schema.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/schema.rs:310): **57 → ~35 LOC**

- `creates_users_table_before_user_email_index`: **KEEP**. Real DDL ordering regression.
- `destructive_pre_alpha_reset_drift_allows_pre_alpha_shape_changes`: **KEEP/COLLAPSE** into a positive/negative classifier table. This supports destructive reset behavior rather than compatibility.

Test section in [`test_support.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/test_support.rs:203): **45 → ~35 LOC**

- Both URL-validation tests: **KEEP**, combine into a table. They prevent tests from destroying a non-test database.

Test section in [`schema_contract/mod.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/schema_contract/mod.rs:212): **81 → ~50 LOC**

- `schema_contract_inventory_covers_all_reset_tables`: **REWRITE**. Its loop is tautological because `metadata_reset_tables()` is built directly from `schema_tables()`. Retain only the meaningful invariant that reset-events remain excluded/append-only.
- `schema_contract_inventory_marks_catalog_row_tables`: **KEEP**. The explicit golden inventory catches accidental omission from catalog persistence.
- `schema_contract_inventory_has_unique_names`: **KEEP/COLLAPSE**. Table name, reset order, and column uniqueness are useful schema-definition invariants.

Test section in [`entities/mod.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/entities/mod.rs:76): **46 → ~35 LOC**

- `persisted_first_push_token_never_stores_plaintext_secret`: **KEEP**. Security boundary.
- `projection_file_uses_bounded_path_key_without_truncating_path`: **KEEP**. Protects long-path persistence correctness.

Test section in [`projection_read_models.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/projection_read_models.rs:241): **105 → ~60 LOC**

- Private-audience and public-audience tests: **COLLAPSE** into one audience/path table.
- Retain both expected results. This is the right persistence/read-model layer for the private projection check, making the API `authorized_collaborator...` test redundant.

Test section in [`metadata_reset.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/metadata_reset.rs:105): **23 → 0 LOC**

- `stale_pre_alpha_reset_is_limited_to_known_visibility_shape_error`: **DELETE**.
- It enshrines compatibility with three stale serialized metadata shapes, directly contrary to the project’s explicit no-legacy/pre-alpha policy. The associated production special case should be considered for deletion separately.

Test section in [`repo_reads.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_reads.rs:883): **27 → ~22 LOC**

- `repo_read_query_skips_aggregate_json_columns`: **KEEP**. It is SQL-string coupled, but it protects a deliberate narrow-query/performance contract that an ordinary behavior test would miss.

Test section in [`request_merges.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/request_merges.rs:177): **155 → ~100 LOC**

- `combined_merge_rolls_back_request_when_repo_mutation_fails`: **KEEP**.
- `combined_merge_commits_repo_and_request_together`: **KEEP**.
- The success/failure pair is strong transaction regression coverage. Reduce only duplicated owner/catalog/request setup.

Test section in [`outbox.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/outbox.rs:463): **20 → ~16 LOC**

- Both retry tests: **KEEP**. Already compact and behaviorally distinct; a small table can trim a few lines.

### Object-store tests

Test section in [`s3.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/object_store/s3.rs:311): **225 → ~145 LOC**

- Signed bucket HEAD readiness test: **KEEP**.
- Signed PUT/GET/DELETE test: **KEEP**.
- Bounded GET rejects oversized declared body before reading: **KEEP**; distinct resource-exhaustion boundary.
- The 150-line raw TCP server is the bulk. Replace it with a small reusable HTTP test server/helper or a mock-server dev dependency. Do not collapse away method/path/body/signature checks.

Test section in [`encrypted.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/object_store/encrypted.rs:118): **30 → ~24 LOC**

- Encryption round-trip/no-plaintext/delete test: **KEEP**. Compact, high-value confidentiality and delegation coverage.

## Concrete delete list

These tests provide no unique, realistic regression protection:

- `app_catalog_starts_empty`
- `unpublished_repo_blocks_public_reads`
- `authorized_collaborator_sees_private_paths`
- `settlement_credit_conversion_failure_does_not_mutate_request_or_account`
- `memory_delete_repo_rejects_refund_balance_above_persisted_range`
- `stale_pre_alpha_reset_is_limited_to_known_visibility_shape_error`

Everything else should mostly be retained as behavioral cases but heavily collapsed through shared scenarios, data tables, and removal of handwritten infrastructure."},"last_task_message":null}]}

The important nuance is that most savings should come from collapsing fixtures and repeated setup, while outright deletion is reserved for tautologies, impossible states, stale compatibility fossils, duplicate delivery-layer assertions, and tests that execute `#[cfg(test)]` alternate code instead of production paths.

The report identifies an approximately 45% safe reduction, concrete deletions, misleading false-green/test-only paths, and the behavioral core to preserve.","plan":[{"step":"Inventory test files, frameworks, line counts, and repository structure","status":"completed"},{"step":"Assign non-overlapping test areas to code-reaper subagents","status":"completed"},{"step":"Trace tests to production behavior and identify redundancy or low-value coverage","status":"completed"},{"step":"Synthesize a prioritized, line-count-aware audit report with no modifications","status":"completed"}]}); text(r); text(p)

content.text:
# Testing audit report

## Verdict

No, this codebase does not need every existing test in its current form.

Using the `code-reaper` standard—every test must justify itself through distinct regression value—the suite can reasonably shrink from approximately **19,101 LOC to 10,544 LOC**, a reduction of about **8,557 lines / 44.8%**, while preserving the meaningful behavioral cases.

Most savings should come from collapsing duplicated fixtures and table-driving related scenarios. Outright deletion should target compatibility fossils, tautologies, unreachable states, duplicated boundary assertions, and tests that execute test-only alternatives instead of production code.

| Area | Current | Target | Reduction |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Core/domain/database | ~4,484 | ~2,237 | 50% |
| API/Git/local-dev | ~11,662 | ~6,833 | 41% |
| CLI/web | ~2,955 | ~1,474 | 50% |
| **Total** | **~19,101** | **~10,544** | **44.8%** |

The current Linux baseline has **438 passing tests and one ignored test**. Execution is fast; maintenance burden and misleading coverage are the problems.

## Most important findings

### 1. Some Git tests do not execute production code

This is more serious than ordinary duplication.

Several helpers exist only under `#[cfg(test)]` and bypass real production orchestration:

- [`credentials.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/credentials.rs:55) defines a test-only credential parser.
- [`artifacts.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/import/artifacts.rs:20) builds pending imports through a test-only path.
- [`import.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/import.rs:27) persists receive-pack updates while bypassing production permission rechecks, promotion, configuration validation, and cleanup.
- [`repo_io.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/import/repo_io.rs:82) exposes another test-only Git-tree path.

Many tests in [`git_receive.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/git_receive.rs:1) therefore provide less protection than their names imply.

These should be rewritten around domain-owned pure behavior and a small number of tests through the actual production entry point. Merely retaining the existing tests preserves false confidence.

### 2. Four database tests report success without testing a database

When `SCOPE_TEST_DATABASE_URL` is absent, three tests in [`repo_lifecycle.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/repo_lifecycle.rs:97) and one in [`db/requests/tests.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/requests/tests.rs:179) return early.

They appear green but execute no Postgres behavior. They should either:

- be explicitly ignored outside a DB job, or
- fail when the DB-designated CI job lacks its database.

Additionally, 11 local-development tests are absent from the default API invocation.

### 3. The architecture test is vastly overbuilt

[`domain_boundary.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/tests/domain_boundary.rs:1) spends 491 lines implementing and testing a handwritten Rust lexer to enforce one dependency rule.

Keep the rule, but reduce it to roughly 60–90 lines using an AST parser. Better still, isolate the domain in a crate whose dependency graph makes the violation impossible, then delete the test entirely.

### 4. Shared test infrastructure is fragile

- `MemoryObjectStore::new()` uses process-global storage, creating potential races between parallel cleanup tests.
- Around 18 Git tests independently create loopback HTTP servers.
- Temporary directories are frequently cleaned up only after successful tests, leaking on panic.
- Several process tests depend on 25ms scheduling windows.
- SSE tests use one-second wall-clock timeouts.
- API tests inherit a 551-line broadly imported “god fixture.”

Use isolated stores, RAII servers/directories, and behavior-local fixtures.

## High-confidence deletions

These tests have no distinct, realistic regression value.

### Compatibility and stale behavior

- Entire [`obsolete_routes.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/obsolete_routes.rs:1).
- Removed revision-route assertion in [`requests.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/requests.rs:871).
- Stale metadata compatibility test in [`metadata_reset.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/metadata_reset.rs:105).
- Legacy CLI flag and prompt-absence tests in [`cli/tests/init.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/cli/tests/init.rs:35).

These directly conflict with the repository’s pre-alpha/no-compatibility policy.

### Tautologies and trivial implementation restatements

- `app_catalog_starts_empty`.
- API empty-test-state test.
- Direct `Principal` field/access tautologies.
- `selected_remote_name` trimming micro-tests.
- CLI `format_user_code` and default directory-name tests.
- Clone URL interpolation tests, including the entire web [`clone-command.test.ts`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/features/repo-detail/clone-command.test.ts:1).
- `canUseRepoLiveRefresh` test—the implementation always returns `true`.
- CLI and review help smoke tests that assert no unique contract.
- Formatter/help-copy substring tests.
- Simple timeout test already subsumed by blocked-stdin and descendant-process tests.

### Duplicate or impossible cases

- `authorized_collaborator_sees_private_paths`: it selects the private projection directly and does not test authorization.
- `unpublished_repo_blocks_public_reads`: a subset of the stronger pending-publish access test.
- Impossible credit/balance overflow states manually constructed beyond persisted ranges.
- Non-chunked first-push round trip duplicated by binary/transport tests.
- Private-binary omission duplicated by content-agnostic projection/privacy coverage.
- Simple valid callback wrapper tests duplicated by the parser/security matrix.
- `push_allows_uncommitted_config`, whose dirty result is actually caused by generated `.gitignore` state.

### Code that is not a test

[`type_exports.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/type_exports.rs:19) is an ignored TypeScript generator masquerading as a test. Rehome it as an explicit task or `xtask`; remove its 167 lines from test accounting.

## Largest consolidation opportunities

### Domain and persistence

- [`domain_projection.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/tests/domain_projection.rs:1): **797 → ~340 LOC**. Retain privacy, reveal, rewrite, baseline, and history-gap semantics; share graphs, commits, events, and assertions.
- [`requests_tests.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/requests_tests.rs:1): **805 → ~390 LOC**. Retain reachable transitions, accounting, atomicity, and settlement behavior through a `RequestScenario`.
- [`repo_visibility_tests.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/repo_visibility_tests.rs:1): **310 → ~170 LOC** using folder, file/error, and canonicalization tables.
- [`db/requests/tests.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/requests/tests.rs:1): **309 → ~175 LOC**. Keep adapter/authorization behavior; stop repeating domain assertions.
- [`object_store/s3.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/object_store/s3.rs:311): **225 → ~145 LOC** by replacing its custom raw TCP server.

### API and Git

Almost every large API file should be collapsed rather than deleted wholesale:

- [`git_http.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/git_http.rs:1): **948 → ~450 LOC**.
- [`repo_lifecycle.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/repo_lifecycle.rs:1): **879 → ~480 LOC**.
- [`git_request_refs.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/git_request_refs.rs:1): **853 → ~500 LOC**.
- [`repo_visibility.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/repo_visibility.rs:1): **786 → ~380 LOC**.
- [`git_receive.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/git_receive.rs:1): **714 → ~420 LOC**, while replacing test-only execution paths.
- [`git_receive_config.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/git_receive_config.rs:1): **555 → ~300 LOC**.
- [`push_intent_completion.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/push_intent_completion.rs:1): **548 → ~330 LOC**.
- [`requests.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/requests.rs:1): **989 → ~580 LOC**.

The intended boundary is: domain tests own projection and transition rules; API tests prove translation, authentication, information hiding, persistence, failure atomicity, and rendered contracts.

### CLI

Four CLI integration files duplicate approximately 400 lines of Git/process/temp-directory harness:

- [`init.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/cli/tests/init.rs:1)
- [`push.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/cli/tests/push.rs:1)
- [`request.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/cli/tests/request.rs:1)
- [`review.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/cli/tests/review.rs:1)

A focused shared harness should save roughly 280–300 lines. Similar duplication exists across `git_repo_tests`, `repo_config_tests`, and request remote tests.

Retain command-specific validation ordering where it prevents authentication, configuration, or filesystem side effects. Collapse the scaffolding, not those distinctions.

### Web

The web suite can shrink from 540 to roughly 269 lines.

- Delete [`clone-command.test.ts`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/features/repo-detail/clone-command.test.ts:1).
- Rewrite [`request-labels.test.ts`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/features/requests/request-labels.test.ts:1): the frontend settlement calculation duplicates the Rust domain and can remain green when backend policy changes.
- Collapse [`cli-callback-handoff.test.ts`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/lib/cli-callback-handoff.test.ts:1) to its security allowlist/parser matrix.
- Keep the mixed binary/text regression in [`review-file-diff-drawer.test.ts`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/features/review/review-file-diff-drawer.test.ts:1).
- Replace low-value live-refresh helper tests with coverage of version coalescing, stale-event rejection, invalidation failure, retry, and abort cleanup—the risky coordinator behavior currently has no tests.

## Tests that must remain

Do not aggressively delete coverage for:

- Visibility, projection, historical privacy, and author redaction.
- Request transitions, settlement accounting, replay/idempotency, and atomic rollback.
- Authentication, token single-use/expiry, callback allowlists, and credential scoping.
- Git binary/CRLF/executable fidelity, gzip/chunking, request refs, stale bases, and cleanup.
- Repository deletion side effects and cleanup-generation fences.
- Encryption, signed S3 operations, and bounded reads.
- Schema ordering, unique schema definitions, and persistence mappings.
- Terminal-injection sanitization and filesystem symlink/traversal protection.

These are real behavioral or security boundaries. Their setup should be shortened, but their semantic cases are valuable.

## Recommended target structure

1. Domain-owned scenario/table tests for invariants and transitions.
2. Thin adapter tests for authorization, translation, persistence, and errors.
3. A deliberately small real-Git suite covering each important transport/storage boundary once.
4. Behavior-local test support modules, isolated stores, and RAII resources.
5. Explicit DB and type-generation jobs—no silent skips or ignored generators posing as tests.

No source files were modified. Two unrelated worktree changes appeared during the audit—`AGENTS.md` and an untracked maintainability-plan document—and were left untouched.
